This prescribes the exact code changes rather than just describing what's broken.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests test_css_loader_extension_constant_exists, test_css_loader_default_in_options, test_loader_from_filename_returns_default, and test_no_regression_in_loaderFromFilename are purely text-matching tests (reading files and checking for string presence). While some tests do execute code (test_repo_css_integration_test, test_esbuild_package_compiles, etc.), the majority of f2p tests are assert-string-in-file patterns.

test_not_tautological.reason: The integration test test_repo_css_integration_test runs the actual Go test TestCSSBuildLoadersDefault which would fail without the real fix applied. A stub implementation couldn't pass the go test and go build checks.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/hugo's solution/solve.sh path in the container (or could be accessed). Also, the agent could grep test_outputs.py to find exact strings like 'defaultCSSFileLoaderExts', 'default to the file loader for common static', etc., and simply insert them into the right files to pass the text-matching tests without understanding the bug.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All six explicit requirements from instruction.md are covered by tests: defaultCSSFileLoaderExts constant (test 1), auto-configure LoaderFile for CSS (test 2), loaderFromFilename returns LoaderDefault (test 3), comment with specific phrase (test 7 checks for it), backward compatibility (test 4/integration), and TestCSSBuildLoadersDefault passing (test 4).
